goal: Add Hard Rule 10 to CLAUDE.md codifying canonical-first edits and forbidding direct www/ or ios/ mirror edits
done: Inserted Hard Rule 10 between Rule 9 and the Architecture map heading in CLAUDE.md. Rule states www/ and ios/ are outputs of cp + cap sync, never inputs, and direct mirror edits cause drift that can reverse live canonical features.
notes: Triggered by 2026-05-28 discovery that www/js/main.js + www/js/data.js had been edited directly (bypassing canonical), reimplementing already-shipped features while stripping canonical-only code — the _meowContinueListenerBound bottom-nav listener in main.js and the resetActiveDeck export in data.js that js/auth.js imports. A naive cp www → js/ would have regressed live production; caught by diff -u js/<file> www/js/<file>. Companion Claude-memory entry feedback_no_mirror_edits.md created outside the repo to surface a diff-first check before future syncs.
